title: Homalium scleroxylon
intro: Homalium scleroxylon, commonly known as African mahogany or Okok, is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Homalium scleroxylon include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Homalium scleroxylon is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make various remedies such as teas, tonics, and ointments.
uses: Homalium scleroxylon is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in West Africa. The plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
